session.user:
You are performing a bulk rename of JSON output field names in Python exec scripts.
IMPORTANT: Only rename STRING KEYS in dicts (like `"plan_url"` and `"plan_title"`) and docstring JSON examples. Do NOT rename Python variable names or function parameters.
For each file, read it first, then apply edits.
### Part A: Rename `"plan_url"` → `"pr_url"` in these files (all under /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/):
1. `plan_update.py` - line 169: `"plan_url": plan_result.url,` → `"pr_url": plan_result.url,` 2. `plan_save.py` - line 348: `"plan_url": result.url,` → `"pr_url": result.url,` 3. `create_impl_context_from_plan.py` - line 89: `"plan_url": plan.url,` → `"pr_url": plan.url,` 4. `setup_impl_from_pr.py` - lines 211, 257, 298: rename `"plan_url"` in dicts 5. `create_pr_from_session.py` - line 12: rename `"plan_url"` in docstring. Line 124: `"plan_url": result.pr_url,` → `"pr_url": result.pr_url,`
### Part B: Rename `"plan_title"` → `"pr_title"` in these files:
1. `setup_impl_from_pr.py` - lines 213, 259, 300: rename `"plan_title"` in dicts 2. `incremental_dispatch.py` - line 167: `"plan_title": pr_result.title,` → `"pr_title": pr_result.title,`
Read each file, then apply edits. Be precise with the old_string to ensure uniqueness.
session.assistant: I'll perform this bulk rename of JSON output field names in the exec scripts. Let me start by reading each file and then applying the edits.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Update an existing plan's content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-update --plan-number N [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→This command updates the plan content comment on an existing GitHub issue: 7→1. Find plan file (from session scratch, --plan-path, or ~/.claude/plans/) 8→2. Update plan content via PlanBackend 9→3. Update issue title from plan H1 heading 10→ 11→Options: 12→ --plan-number N: Plan number to update (required) 13→ --session-id ID: Session ID to find plan file in scratch storage 14→ --plan-path PATH: Direct path to plan file (overrides session lookup) 15→ 16→Output: 17→ --format json (default): {"success": true, ...} 18→ --format display: Formatted text 19→ 20→Exit Codes: 21→ 0: Success - plan comment updated 22→ 1: Error - plan not found, no plan found, etc. 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk_shared.context.helpers import (
... [151 lines omitted] ...
37→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 83→ def _handle_update_error(error_msg: str, cause: Exception | None = None) -> None: 84→ """Output error message and exit with code 1.""" 86→ click.echo(f"Error: {error_msg}", err=True) 88→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": error_msg})) 106→ _handle_update_error("No plan found in ~/.claude/plans/") 115→ _handle_update_error(f"Plan #{plan_number} not found") 123→ except RuntimeError as e: 124→ _handle_update_error(f"Failed to update comment: {e}", cause=e) 133→ except RuntimeError as e: 134→ _handle_update_error(f"Failed to update title: {e}", cause=e) 152→ branch_updated = not isinstance(push_result, PushError) 162→ click.echo(f"Warning: branch push to {branch_name} failed")

1→"""Plan save: creates a draft PR with the plan content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-save [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→Options: 7→ --format json|display: Output format (default: json) 8→ --plan-file PATH: Use specific plan file (highest priority) 9→ --session-id ID: Session ID for scoped plan lookup 10→ --plan-type standard|learn: Plan type (default: standard) 11→ --learned-from-issue INT: Parent plan (for learn plans) 12→ --created-from-workflow-run-url URL: Workflow run URL 13→ 14→Objective linking: use --objective to pass directly, or rely on 15→automatic linking via the objective-context session marker 16→(created by /erk:objective-plan). The --objective flag takes 17→precedence over the session marker. 18→ 19→Exit Codes: 20→ 0: Success 21→ 1: Error 22→ 2: Validation failed 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.validate_plan_content import _validate_plan_content
... [558 lines omitted] ...
162→ click.echo(f"Error: {title_validation.message}", err=True) 168→ "error": f"Plan title validation failed: {title_validation.reason}", 169→ "error_type": "validation_failed", 183→ click.echo("Error: --current-branch requires a checked-out branch.", err=True) 190→ "Error: --branch-slug is required. " 222→ click.echo(f"Error: {create_result.message}", err=True) 316→ raise RuntimeError(msg) 420→ click.echo("Error: No plan found in ~/.claude/plans/", err=True) 422→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": "No plan found in ~/.claude/plans/"})) 452→ valid, error, details = _validate_plan_content(plan) 455→ click.echo(f"Error: Plan validation failed: {error}", err=True) 461→ "error": f"Plan validation failed: {error}", 462→ "error_type": "validation_failed",

1→"""Create .erk/impl-context/ folder from plan content. 2→ 3→This exec command fetches a plan via PlanBackend and creates the .erk/impl-context/ 4→folder structure, providing a testable alternative to inline workflow scripts. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan <plan-id> 8→ 9→Output: 10→ Structured JSON output with success status and folder details 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Success (.erk/impl-context/ folder created) 14→ 1: Error (plan not found, fetch failed, folder creation failed) 15→ 16→Examples: 17→ $ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan 1028 18→ {"success": true, "impl_context_path": "/path/to/.erk/impl-context", "plan_id": 1028} 19→ 20→ $ erk exec create-impl-context-from-plan 999 21→ {"success": false, "error": "plan_not_found", "message": "..."} 22→""" 23→ 24→import json 25→ 26→import click 27→ 28→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 29→ require_plan_backend, 30→ require_repo_root,
... [67 lines omitted] ...
61→ error_output = { 63→ "error": "plan_not_found", 67→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_output), err=True)

1→"""Set up .erk/impl-context/ folder from GitHub PR in current worktree. 2→ 3→This exec command fetches a plan from a GitHub PR, creates a feature branch, 4→checks it out, and creates the .erk/impl-context/ folder structure for implementation. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec setup-impl-from-pr <plan-number> [--session-id <id>] 8→ 9→Output: 10→ Structured JSON output with success status and folder details 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Success (.erk/impl-context/ folder created, branch checked out) 14→ 1: Error (PR not found, plan fetch failed, git operations failed) 15→ 16→Examples: 17→ $ erk exec setup-impl-from-pr 1028 18→ {"success": true, "impl_path": "/path/to/.erk/impl-context", 19→ "plan_number": 1028, "branch": "P1028-..."} 20→""" 21→ 22→import json 23→from pathlib import Path 24→ 25→import click 26→ 27→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 28→ require_branch_manager, 29→ require_cwd, 30→ require_git,
... [324 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Dispatch a local plan against an existing PR for remote implementation. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec incremental-dispatch --plan-file <path> --pr <number> [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→Commits the plan as impl-context to the PR's branch and triggers the 7→plan-implement workflow. Unlike regular dispatch, this does not require 8→the erk-plan label — just an OPEN PR. 9→""" 10→ 11→import json 12→from pathlib import Path 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_cmd import load_workflow_config 17→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced, sync_branch_to_sha 18→from erk.cli.commands.pr.metadata_helpers import write_dispatch_metadata 19→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 20→from erk.cli.constants import DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME 21→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 22→ require_branch_manager, 23→ require_context, 24→ require_git, 25→ require_github, 26→ require_plan_backend, 27→ require_repo_root, 28→ require_time, 29→) 30→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel
... [208 lines omitted] ...
31→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 85→ _output_error(output_format, f"PR #{pr_number} not found") 89→ _output_error(output_format, f"PR #{pr_number} is {pr_result.state}, must be OPEN") 100→ _output_error(output_format, "Not in a git repository") 149→ if isinstance(push_result, PushError): 150→ _output_error(output_format, f"Push failed: {push_result.message}") 197→ except RuntimeError as e: 199→ click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Failed to update dispatch metadata: {e}" 227→def _output_error(output_format: str, message: str) -> None: 228→ """Output error in the requested format.""" 230→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": message})) 232→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + message)

=== create_pr_from_session.py - docstring === Output: JSON result on stdout: {"success": true, "pr_number": N, "pr_url": "...", "branch_name": "..."} Error messages on stderr with exit code 1 on failure
=== create_pr_from_session.py - line 124 === # Return success result output = { "success": True, "pr_number": result.pr_number, "pr_url": result.pr_url, "branch_name": result.branch_name, "title": result.title, } click.echo(json.dumps(output))

=== incremental_dispatch.py - line 167 === inputs = { "pr_number": str(pr_number), "submitted_by": submitted_by, "pr_title": pr_result.title, "branch_name": branch_name, "pr_number": str(pr_number), "base_branch": trunk, "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "dispatch_type": "incremental", **workflow_config, }
